instruction: Read the given notes carefully. Write a short paragraph of 35-45 words using the information provided. Organise your ideas clearly and form complete sentences. Ensure your response is relevant, coherent, and grammatically correct.
question:
Notes:
last Sunday: visited grandparents
helped in the garden
ate lunch together
heard old stories
felt relaxed
expected_answer: Last Sunday, I visited my grandparents and helped them in the garden. We ate lunch together and spent time talking. They told me some old stories, and I felt relaxed and happy throughout the day.
explanation: The answer uses all the notes, forms a coherent paragraph, stays within the expected length, and maintains clear grammar.

instruction: Rearrange the words/phrases to form a correct sentence. Begin with a capital letter and end with a full stop.
question: carefully / the / listened / students / teacher / to / the
expected_answer: The students listened carefully to the teacher.
explanation: The words are arranged into a grammatically correct sentence with proper capitalisation and punctuation.

instruction: Read the situation. Write the reply in one sentence.
question:
Situation: Your friend has invited you to a birthday party, but you already have an exam the next morning. You want to decline politely.

Message: 'Please come to my birthday party this evening.'
expected_answer: Thank you for inviting me, but I cannot come to your birthday party this evening because I have an exam tomorrow morning.
explanation: The expected answer politely declines the invitation in one sentence and gives a clear reason.

instruction: Write a short paragraph (35-45 words) using the notes. Use complete sentences.
question:
Notes:
this morning: missed the bus
walked to school quickly
reached a little late
apologised to teacher
promised to wake up earlier
expected_answer: This morning, I missed the bus, so I walked to school quickly. I reached a little late and apologised to my teacher. I promised myself that I would wake up earlier from tomorrow.
explanation: The response uses all the notes, presents them in logical order, and forms a coherent paragraph.

instruction: Read the given notes carefully. Write a short paragraph of 35-45 words using the information provided. Organise your ideas clearly and form complete sentences. Ensure your response is relevant, coherent, and grammatically correct.

instruction: Read the situation. Write the reply in one sentence.

explanation: The reply answers the message politely in one sentence and clearly states when the book will be returned.

instruction: Write a short paragraph (35-45 words) using the notes. Use complete sentences.
question:

Friday evening: community library visit
borrowed two storybooks

wanted to visit again
expected_answer: On Friday evening, I visited the community library and borrowed two storybooks. I sat quietly and read for an hour. I also met the librarian, who was very helpful. The visit was enjoyable, and I wanted to go there again.
explanation: The paragraph includes all the points from the notes, uses complete sentences, and stays suitable for the task.
